page: Microsoft Azure D64pds_v5 is an ARM64-based server powered by an Ampere Altra ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor. It features 64 dedicated physical cores and 64 vCPUs running at 3.0 GHz, with 1.0 thread per core. The memory configuration provides 208.0 GB of RAM, translating to 3.25 GB per core. It includes 2458 GB of local SSD storage and is Premium Storage capable, operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates strong multi-core performance, highlighted by a stress-ng multi-core score of 112,395.52 and a multi-threaded bzip3 decompression rate of 414.45 GB/sec. Memory bandwidth for cached small blocks is highly efficient at 4,383.55 GB/sec, though large-block uncached read and write speeds fall into the average tier. Single-core performance is relatively weaker, as evidenced by lower single-core Geekbench and bzip3 decompression scores. However, parallelized workloads like software compilation with Clang and ray tracing show strong results.

The ARM-based architecture of the DPDSv5 family offers high physical core density, making it a qualitatively cost-effective option for multi-threaded workloads compared to traditional x86 architectures. With 2458 GB of local storage and strong Redis SET performance reaching 82.63 million operations per second, this server is well-suited for database deployments, multi-threaded web serving, software compilation pipelines, and parallel data processing tasks that benefit from dedicated physical cores without hyperthreading.
description: Microsoft Azure D64pds_v5 is an ARM64-based virtual machine powered by an Ampere Altra Neoverse-N1 processor with 64 dedicated physical cores and 64 vCPUs running at 3.0 GHz. It features 208.0 GB of RAM and 2458 GB of local storage, operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The server delivers strong multi-core performance, as shown by its high multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and stress-ng multi-core benchmark results. It also excels in memory bandwidth for small cached blocks and Redis SET operations, which reach 82.63 million operations per second. While single-core performance is moderate, the high physical core density provides a qualitatively cost-efficient platform for highly parallelized workloads. This server is optimized for multi-threaded applications, database hosting, software compilation, and scale-out web serving.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D64pds_v5 is an ARM64 server featuring 64 dedicated Ampere Neoverse-N1 cores, 208 GB of RAM, and 2458 GB of local storage, delivering strong multi-core performance for parallel workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D64pds_v5 is an ARM64 server with 64 dedicated Ampere cores, 208 GB RAM, and 2458 GB local storage, optimized for multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A high-density ARM64 server featuring sixty-four dedicated physical cores and local storage for highly parallelized multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated physical ARM64 Ampere Neoverse-N1 cores at 3.0 GHz
208.0 GB of system memory and 2458 GB of local SSD storage
Strong multi-core performance and high cached memory bandwidth
Excellent throughput for Redis SET operations and software compilation
Best suited for multi-threaded databases, web serving, and parallel processing
